title: Perl Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Perl source files across all three common roles: executable scripts (`.pl`), reusable modules (`.pm`), and Plain Old Documentation files (`.pod`). Perl is widely used for text processing, system administration, and legacy web applications.

## When to use
Use `media:perl;textable;code` when a pipeline node consumes or produces Perl source — for example, a linting step using `perlcritic`, a module packaging step for CPAN, or a legacy automation script runner.

## Notes
POD files (`.pod`) contain structured documentation embedded in or alongside Perl code and are parsed by tools like `perldoc`. All three extensions share the same media type since they use identical syntax. See also [Perl Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:perl;textable;code).
